_meta.purpose: Catalog of worker models the orchestrator can delegate to via OpenCode. Refresh prices quarterly from https://models.dev.
_meta.notes: Model IDs use OpenRouter format `openrouter/<provider>/<model>` (verified against `opencode models` 2026-04-30). Prices approximate. Use the catalog as data — never hardcode IDs in scripts.

code-cheap.rationale: code specialist, far cheaper than Sonnet, strong on refactors / boilerplate

code-frontier.rationale: when reasoning over a large change matters; reach for this only when qwen-coder doesn't cut it

code-quick.rationale: single-file edits, no architectural reasoning needed

summarize.rationale: long-context cheap; great for transcripts and long docs

summarize-fast.rationale: short text where Kimi's context is overkill

reasoning-cheap.rationale: explicit chain-of-thought at a fraction of frontier prices

reasoning-deep.rationale: use sparingly — only when nothing else will do; consumes Anthropic quota

translate.rationale: Qwen excels at non-English tasks; cheap

extract.rationale: structured JSON extraction is Haiku's sweet spot

kimi.use_when: transcripts, long doc analysis, bulk summarization

qwen-coder.use_when: code generation, refactor, boilerplate, autocomplete-style tasks

qwen.use_when: drafting, translation, general writing where cost matters

glm.use_when: default for drafts and routine work — when in doubt, GLM

minimax.use_when: multi-document analysis, repo-wide reads, anything past 256k tokens

deepseek-r1.use_when: needs explicit reasoning trace, math, planning a multi-step approach cheaply

haiku.use_when: structured extraction, classification, anything where you need predictable shape

claude-sonnet.use_when: code work where qwen-coder has tried and failed

claude-opus.use_when: ONLY when no cheaper model will do; you yourself are Opus, so calling this is rare

gpt5-mini.use_when: OpenAI-flavored output preferred, or as a sanity-check second opinion

gemini-flash.use_when: long-context with images/PDFs; fast bulk reads
